The board Of public works Of the
city of Spokane will receive bids in
duplicate, until March II, 1901. at the
hour Of O'Clock p. m. for the i'm
provement Of First avenue from the
east line of Madison street to the
east line of Walnut street by paving.
guttering. curbing. according to the
plans and specifications on file in the
office of the city engineer, and re-
ferred to in Ordinance No. A144S.
which ordinance and said map. plans
and specifications shall govern said
are hereby referred for further par
ticulars. All bids must be made in
duplicate on printed forms to be fur
nished by the board. sealed. and one
copy left with the clerk of the board.
the other with the mayor.


There must be written upon the


outside of the envelope the words.
Proposal for the paving of First
avenue" together with the name Of


the bidder. and the copy of the bid
panied with a certified check for not


less than 10 per cent of the amount of


the bid and made out to the order of
the president of the board of public


works. and no bid shall be considered


unless accompanied by said check.


The successful bidder shall be re-
quired to enter into a contract and
furnish satisfactory bonds as reQuir-
ed by said Ordinance No. ALAS. the
laws of the state Of Washington, the
city charter and ordinance pertaining
upon the subject within five days
after ne has been notified that the
contract has been awarded to him.


Special attention is called to Ordi-


nance No. A1031. governing contracts
for public work. Where any material


here the same shall be of home pro-
duction when prices and quality are
equal to foreign production.
The board reserves the right to re-


tect any and all bids. Successful
bidder on the above proposal will be
| required to furnish a guarantee or


indemnity company bond satisfactory
to the mayor.


MAINTENANCE


Bids In the manner and under the
conditions and at the time above
stated will be received by the board
of public works for maintaining the
above described pavement and i'm
provement in the manner and accord
| ing to the terms and conditions stated


under the maintenance portion of the


plans and specifications for a period
Of ten years from and after the com
pletion OF the said work and its ac-
| ceptance by the city. and the sue
cessful bidder will be required to


Is required by law and charter and
ordinance of sSlu city. and particular
ly as required by said Ordinance No.
144S. within five days after he shall


have been notified of the fact that
he has been awarded the said CON
tract


Such proposals shall be made In
duplicate and in sealed envelopes
marked Proposals for Maintaining


First Avenue Pavement" and shall


be accompanied by certified check


for not less than 10 per cent of the


amount OF the bid. made out to the


order of the president OF the board


OF public works.


The board expressly reserves the


right to reject any and all bids


l.. b. HANDLEY. Secretary.
